Package io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.transport

Class WsTransport

java.lang.Object
io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.transport.AbstractTransport
io.mdudel.zenoh.purejava.transport.WsTransport
All Implemented Interfaces:
Transport, AutoCloseable
public final class WsTransport extends AbstractTransport
WebSocket implementation of Transport for the Zenoh 1.x publisher, backed by the JDK's built-in WebSocket (JDK 11+). Supports both ws:// and wss:// URIs; the latter requires a TlsConfig to source an SSLContext.

Framing. WebSocket is message-oriented: each sendBinary(buf, true) emits one binary frame on the wire and each onBinary callback receives one binary frame. Because of that, this transport does not use StreamFramer — the WebSocket wire already delimits messages. Callers still see whole batches in and out via the Transport SPI, exactly as they do with TcpTransport or TlsTransport.

WebSocket-level message fragmentation (an onBinary call with last=false) is handled transparently: fragments are accumulated into a temporary buffer and only delivered to the inbox once the FIN bit is observed. The accumulated size is capped at StreamFramer.MAX_FRAME_BYTES to match the Zenoh wire cap.

Threading:

  • AbstractTransport.send(byte[]) calls WebSocket.sendBinary(ByteBuffer, boolean) and blocks on the returned CompletableFuture under a per-instance monitor, so concurrent publishers serialise on the same WebSocket exactly as they do on the same TCP socket.
  • Receive callbacks come in on the JDK HttpClient's internal executor and delegate to AbstractTransport.deliver(byte[]). The caller thread that calls AbstractTransport.receive(long, TimeUnit) still blocks on the inbox.

Zero third-party dependencies. Everything is JDK stdlib.

  • Constructor Details

    • WsTransport

      public WsTransport(URI uri, TlsConfig tls)
      Parameters:
      uri - ws://host:port or wss://host:port (path optional; Zenoh 1.x uses root)
      tls - required for wss; must be null for ws

  • Method Details

    • setConnectTimeoutMs

      public WsTransport setConnectTimeoutMs(int ms)
      Underlying HTTP connect timeout in ms. Must be called before AbstractTransport.connect().

    • setOperationTimeoutMs

      public WsTransport setOperationTimeoutMs(int ms)
      Await-timeout for handshake, send, and close operations. Must be called before AbstractTransport.connect().

    • doConnect

      protected void doConnect() throws TransportException
      Description copied from class: AbstractTransport
      Open the underlying link. Called exactly once by AbstractTransport.connect() under the once-only guard. Subclasses arrange for received batches to be delivered via AbstractTransport.deliver(byte[]), and for asynchronous failures to be reported via AbstractTransport.reportReaderError(Throwable).
      Specified by:
      doConnect in class AbstractTransport
      Throws:
      TransportException - on connect failure (opened flag is cleared by AbstractTransport.connect() before rethrow)

    • doSend

      protected void doSend(byte[] batch) throws TransportException
      Description copied from class: AbstractTransport
      Write one batch synchronously. Called under the caller thread; the caller does NOT hold any internal lock at this point. Subclasses may serialise on their own writer or rely on their transport primitive being thread-safe.
      Specified by:
      doSend in class AbstractTransport
      Throws:
      TransportException

    • doClose

      protected void doClose()
      Description copied from class: AbstractTransport
      Release resources. Called at most once; the atomic-CAS in AbstractTransport.close() guarantees no re-entrance.
      Specified by:
      doClose in class AbstractTransport

    • describe

      public String describe()
      Description copied from interface: Transport
      Human-readable description of the peer endpoint, e.g. "tcp/10.0.0.1:7447".
